title: 补齐供应链 SBOM 与 hash 锁定语义

sources.reason: R3 supply-chain completeness requirement and known boundary.
SBOM parser and lock semantics implementation.
Published supply-chain capability and limitation claims.
Stable direct dependency input for reproducible lock generation.
Release metadata carries digests for generated supply-chain evidence.

riskAssessment.reason: Supply-chain claims must not exceed reproducibly generated evidence.

executionDecision.reason: Inspect and improve reproducible SBOM/lock evidence without claiming unavailable transitive coverage.

preReviewWarnings: Do not claim complete transitive dependency or hash-lock coverage without reproducible evidence.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Workflow actions and direct lock entries remain fully represented in the SBOM.
Transitive dependency and hash-pin capabilities are either implemented with reproducible evidence or explicitly fail closed and documented.
Generated SBOM/provenance and release digests remain internally consistent.
Focused supply-chain tests and full project/governance checks pass.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.

intent.problem: The current SBOM records workflow actions and direct version pins but explicitly lacks transitive dependency generation and artifact hash pins.
constraints: Do not publish or push a release.
intent.rationale: Supply-chain documentation must accurately reflect what can be reproduced and verified from repository inputs.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: All workflow uses occurrences and direct lock entries are represented in generated SBOM evidence.
Unsupported transitive/hash guarantees are rejected or explicitly surfaced in metadata and docs.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly approved continuing N5 to complete the supply-chain remediation, including CI and generated evidence paths.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
